title: Browser-visible runtime labels and permissive preview notes broaden exposure review
impact_summary: Frontend bundles and public runtime values can reveal operational detail, hostnames, and allowlist intent that deserve review.
why_this_matters: Config review often spans both code and browser-visible artifacts, which makes it a useful breadth story in demos.
evidence_fixture: frontend bundle snapshot and safe preview-origin placeholders
suggested_patch_summary: Keep public runtime labels minimal, narrow allowlists, and move operational detail out of browser-visible config.
verification_summary: Verified as a reviewer-owned narrative because all runtime values are synthetic placeholders and no live CORS policy exists.
replay_regression_note: Confirm browser-visible values stay synthetic and the disabled client-exposed config snippet never becomes runtime code.
